title: Piquant Nutty Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Algerian dish featuring lamb, nuts, and a blend of spices, with a unique cheese-pull effect, this recipe is a crowd-pleaser, and its use of white wine vinegar and beurre-monte gives it a distinct taste, while being ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced almonds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the white wine vinegar, beurre-monte, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the crumbled feta cheese and cook for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the sauce is creamy.
